mission.title: opencost: Failed OpenCost integration to GCP
mission.description: Failed OpenCost integration to GCP . Community-requested feature.

steps.title: Check current opencost deployment
steps.description:
Verify your opencost version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n opencost -l app.kubernetes.io/name=opencost
helm list -n opencost 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working opencost installation.

steps.title: Review opencost configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant opencost configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n opencost -l app.kubernetes.io/name=opencost
kubectl get configmap -n opencost -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=opencost
```
Following step by step the installation guide, Opencost 1.110 fails the integration to GCP.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Failed OpenCost integration to GCP
steps.description:
## What does this PR change?
This PR adds a memory backed config controller which can be used when there is no PV to back the cloud config controllers state. As is this is the only way the config controller will be run in OpenCost. 
Doing this prevents errors such as
```yaml
2024-05-14T07:36:43.43448165Z WRN Controller: pullWatchers: failed to load config statuses from file: open /var/configs/cloud-configurations.json: no such file or directory. Proceeding to create the file
2024-05-14T07:36:43.43465676Z ERR Controller: pullWatchers: failed to save statuses failed to save config statuses to file: open /var/configs/cloud-configurations.json: no such file or directory
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n opencost -l app.kubernetes.io/name=opencost
kubectl get events -n opencost --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Failed OpenCost integration to GCP " is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
## What does this PR change?
This PR adds a memory backed config controller which can be used when there is no PV to back the cloud config controllers state. As is this is the only way the config controller will be run in OpenCost.

2024-05-14T07:36:43.43465676Z ERR Controller: pullWatchers: failed to save statuses failed to save config statuses to file: open /var/configs/cloud-configurations.json: no such file or directory
2024-06-07T19:33:57.065968538Z ERR Controller: pullWatchers: failed to save statuses failed to save config statuses to file: open /var/configs/cloud-configurations.json: permission denied

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with opencost installed or the issue environment reproducible.
